_note: MOCK procedure result. No real patient. For PreChart development/eval. Result of the Barrett's surveillance EGD PT-048 was referred for.

procedure.name: Esophagogastroduodenoscopy (EGD) with Barrett's surveillance biopsies
procedure.indication: Surveillance of Barrett's esophagus (C2M4, no prior dysplasia); asymptomatic, scheduled surveillance due
procedure.prep_quality: N/A (upper endoscopy); patient NPO >8 hours, adequate

procedure_report: PROCEDURE: Esophagogastroduodenoscopy with Barrett's surveillance biopsies. INDICATION: Surveillance of known Barrett's esophagus (C2M4, no dysplasia on 2024 biopsies); patient asymptomatic. ANTICOAGULATION: Patient on apixaban 5 mg BID; last dose held 2 days pre-procedure per periprocedural plan (low thrombotic risk per hematology, no bridging). SEDATION: Moderate sedation, well tolerated. FINDINGS: The endoscope was passed under direct vision to the second portion of the duodenum. ESOPHAGUS: Salmon-colored columnar-lined mucosa extending in a tongue-like fashion in the distal esophagus, Prague classification C2M4, unchanged from prior. No visible nodularity, ulceration, or mucosal irregularity. No suspicious lesion for targeted biopsy. Four-quadrant biopsies obtained every 2 cm per Seattle protocol. Z-line at 38 cm, diaphragmatic hiatus at 40 cm (small hiatal hernia). STOMACH: Normal mucosa, no ulcer or mass; retroflexion normal. DUODENUM: Normal bulb and second portion. No active bleeding at any point. IMPRESSION: Barrett's esophagus C2M4, no visible dysplasia or nodularity, stable appearance. Small hiatal hernia. Seattle-protocol surveillance biopsies obtained; pathology pending. No complications.

findings.finding: Barrett's esophagus, Prague C2M4, no visible dysplasia or nodularity, stable vs 2024

specimens.site: distal esophagus — Barrett's segment, Seattle protocol

disposition: Discharged home same day in stable condition with escort. Post-procedure and sedation precautions reviewed. Resume normal diet. Continue omeprazole 40 mg daily.

followup.recommendation: Review Barrett's surveillance biopsy pathology; call patient with result and set surveillance interval (repeat EGD ~3 years if no dysplasia)
Resume apixaban 5 mg BID per periprocedural plan given no bleeding and low-bleeding-risk procedure
Reconcile active medication list: discontinue warfarin, add apixaban 5 mg BID (per hematology 2026-03-10); ensure future periprocedural plans reference apixaban, not warfarin/INR

planted_followup_items.description: Barrett's Seattle-protocol surveillance biopsy pathology pending — result must be communicated to patient and drive the surveillance interval; dysplasia would escalate management
planted_followup_items.expected_action: call patient with pathology result and document surveillance interval

planted_followup_items.description: Apixaban resumption after EGD with biopsies; the DOAC was truly held 2 days pre-procedure and must be restarted per the periprocedural plan (patient is on apixaban, not warfarin as charted)
planted_followup_items.expected_action: confirm safe resumption timing of apixaban 5 mg BID

planted_followup_items.description: Set Barrett's surveillance interval (repeat EGD ~3 years if no dysplasia) contingent on pathology
planted_followup_items.expected_action: document next surveillance EGD date after pathology returns

planted_followup_items.description: Active medication list still shows warfarin as active while patient is actually on apixaban 5 mg BID (hematology switch 2026-03-10 never reconciled); stale January INR should not guide anticoagulation. This is a med-list correction (discontinue warfarin, add apixaban), distinct from the actual apixaban resumption captured in F2.
planted_followup_items.expected_action: discontinue warfarin and add apixaban on the med list; remove reliance on stale INR

post_procedure_followup._note: Patient-reported stream after the procedure — the inbound signal the LeftBehind product triages. Each contact is labeled ground truth so triage can be scored.

contacts.verbatim: My throat feels a bit scratchy and I'm burping a lot with some bloating. Is that normal after the camera?
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-EGD sore throat and insufflation-related bloating/belching
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; mild sore throat and bloating/gas are expected after upper endoscopy and settle within 24-48h. Warm saline gargles, light diet.
contacts.rationale: Classic benign post-EGD symptoms with no red-flag features; self-care only.

contacts.verbatim: When will I hear back about the biopsies, and did I restart the apixaban at the right time? I took it the evening after the scope.
contacts.clinical_signal: Question about pending Barrett's pathology and confirming appropriate apixaban resumption timing
contacts.expected_action: Answer from the result: Seattle-protocol biopsies pending (expected ~day 4-5, 2026-08-03), GI will call with the result and surveillance interval. Confirm apixaban restart the evening after a bleed-free low-risk EGD is per plan (F2) — timing was correct.
contacts.rationale: Fully answerable from the procedure result and follow-up plan; links to the pending_pathology item and confirms the med_resume timing was appropriate (no escalation needed).

contacts.verbatim: I noticed a tiny bit of pink when I brushed my teeth this morning. No throat pain otherwise and I feel fine. Should I worry?
contacts.clinical_signal: Trace blood-tinged saliva after biopsy, no dysphagia/odynophagia, no hematemesis, hemodynamically well — benign minor oropharyngeal/biopsy-site trace, not GI bleeding
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; a trace of pink in saliva a few days after Seattle-protocol biopsies is expected and self-limited. Return precautions: vomiting blood, black/tarry stools, chest pain, or trouble swallowing — call/seek care immediately.
contacts.rationale: Dangerous-sounding-but-benign: minor trace pink after biopsy in an otherwise-well patient does not indicate significant bleeding; reassure with clear return precautions rather than escalate.
